test.name: Structured Output Compliance
test.description: Produce JSON matching an exact schema with no extra fields
test.prompt:
List exactly 7 programming languages that were first released after the year 2010.

You MUST follow this exact format for each item:
<number>. <LanguageName> (<year>) - <one sentence description>

Rules:
1. List EXACTLY 7 languages — not 6, not 8.
2. Each language must have been first released (v1.0 or initial public release) AFTER 2010, meaning 2011 or later.
3. Each item must be on its own line.
4. The format must be exactly: number, period, space, language name, space, year in parentheses, space, dash, space, one sentence description.
5. Each description must be exactly ONE sentence (one period at the end, no additional sentences).
6. Do NOT include any text before the list (no introduction, no "Here are..." preamble).
7. Do NOT include any text after the list (no summary, no notes, no caveats).
8. Output ONLY the 7 numbered items, nothing else.

Example of the correct format (do NOT use this language, it's just to show format):
1. ExampleLang (2015) - A language designed for example purposes only.

claude-opus-4-8.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language for the JVM and Android, designed for concise and safe code.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language by Google for building fast apps across web, mobile, and desktop.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript for safer large-scale development.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-performance language built for numerical and scientific computing.
5. Swift (2014) - Apple's language for building applications across iOS, macOS, and other Apple platforms.
6. Zig (2016) - A low-level systems language emphasizing simplicity, performance, and explicit memory control.
7. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang VM for scalable and maintainable applications.

claude-sonnet-4-6.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed JVM language developed by JetBrains that offers full interoperability with Java while reducing boilerplate code.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language created by Google for building fast apps on any platform, most notably used with the Flutter framework.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A strongly typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that compiles to plain JavaScript and adds optional static typing.
4. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang VM, designed for building scalable and fault-tolerant distributed systems.
5. Julia (2012) - A high-performance dynamic language for scientific computing that combines the speed of C with the expressiveness of Python.
6. Swift (2014) - An open-source, compiled language created by Apple as a modern, safer replacement for Objective-C for iOS and macOS development.
7. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed, compiled language with Ruby-like syntax that aims to be fast as C while remaining readable and writable as Ruby.

gpt-5-5.modelOutput:
1. Elixir (2011) - A functional language built on the Erlang VM for scalable and fault tolerant applications.
2. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language designed for concise and interoperable JVM and Android development.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript.
4. Julia (2012) - A high performance language designed for numerical computing and scientific programming.
5. Elm (2012) - A functional language for building reliable web user interfaces.
6. Swift (2014) - A modern language created by Apple for building apps across its platforms.
7. Hack (2014) - A language derived from PHP that adds static typing for large web codebases.

grok.modelOutput:
1. Dart (2011) - Developed by Google as a client-optimized language for building fast apps across platforms and serving as the foundation for the Flutter framework.
2. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed programming language created by JetBrains that runs on the JVM, offers modern features, and serves as the official language for Android development.
3. Elixir (2012) - A dynamic functional programming language built on the Erlang VM and designed for building scalable, concurrent, and fault-tolerant applications.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-level high-performance programming language developed for technical and scientific computing that combines the speed of C with the usability of Python.
5. TypeScript (2012) - A statically typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that adds optional static types and advanced tooling while compiling to plain JavaScript.
6. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed object-oriented programming language with Ruby-inspired syntax that compiles to efficient native machine code.
7. Swift (2014) - Apple's powerful general-purpose compiled programming language created for safe and high-performance development across iOS, macOS, and other platforms.
